calendar.matters:
Motion For Relief From Government Entity Claim Requirements [Gov. Code 945.4, 946.6]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ZACK V. MULJAT, Esq. of Gammill Law (Tel: 310-750-4149) for petitioner Ralph F. Gescat;
Atty. DAVID M. PERL, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (Tel: 213-891-9100) for Respondent Housing Authority of the City and County of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER RALPH GESCAT, AS THE SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST AND PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF RICHARD BOYD STAPP GESCAT, DECEASED Motion For Relief From Government Entity Claim Requirements [Gov. Code 945.4, 946.6].

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner's motion for relief from the government claims requirement is granted. Petitioner demonstrates excusable neglect under Government Code section 946.6 and respondents fail to demonstrate prejudice to warrant the denial of relief.

"Section 946.6 is a remedial statute intended to provide relief from technical rules that otherwise provide a trap for the unwary claimant. As such, it is construed in favor of relief whenever possible." (Bettencourt v. Los Rios Community College Dist. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 270, 275.) Here, petitioner attempted to procure counsel during the claims period and was rebuffed. He also experienced severe emotional distress and was waiting to figure out the cause of the fire. (Gescat Decl., pars. 4, 9-12; Alvarez Decl., 7-8.) Petitioner demonstrates good cause for relief and respondents fail to demonstrate prejudice.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
